item.title: Hardcoded AWS AMI IDs in aws.ts will become stale and silently break EC2 provisioning
item.intent: EC2 provisioning must work in any supported AWS region with the current Ubuntu 24.04 LTS AMI. Hardcoded AMI IDs are a time bomb — runtime lookup is required.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts lines 37-42 contain a hardcoded map of Ubuntu 24.04 AMI IDs per AWS region (e.g. 'us-east-1': 'ami-0c7217cdde317cfec'). AWS periodically updates these AMIs and the old IDs become invalid, causing provisioning to fail with an opaque EC2 error. Only 4 of ~25 AWS regions are covered — users in other regions fall back silently to the us-east-1 AMI which may not work in their region. Fix: add a runtime AMI lookup using the AWS EC2 DescribeImages API to fetch the current Ubuntu 24.04 LTS AMI for the target region at provision time. Fall back to hardcoded values only if the API call fails, and log a warning.
acceptanceCriteria: AWS provisioning resolves the current Ubuntu 24.04 LTS AMI for the target region at runtime via EC2 DescribeImages
Provisioning works in AWS regions not in the old hardcoded map
If DescribeImages fails, falls back to hardcoded map and logs a visible warning
